mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the unemployment rate signals a tight labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for UNRATE remains positive or between -0.02 and 0.02 over any two-year rolling window, or the average forward return following UNRATE levels below 4% is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 3.5%, the cost of capital increases sharply, reducing corporate investment and consumer spending, which depresses equity prices over the following week as growth expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: Forward five-day returns after a FEDFUNDS crossing above 3.5% are not on average negative with a t-statistic less than -1.5, or the signal fails to produce a positive hit rate relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts remain elevated above 1500 thousand units while the federal funds rate is above 3%, the housing market is overheated and monetary policy is tightening, creating a joint signal that the economy is nearing a peak, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal's average forward 20-day return is not significantly different from the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10) or the interaction term in a linear model has an inconsistent sign across five-year subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: During recessionary periods defined by unemployment above 5.5%, increases in nonfarm payroll employment signal a nascent recovery that boosts investor sentiment and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the following three months.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, the correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward 60-day returns is not positive (Pearson r <= 0) or the strategy of buying after positive payroll surprises in this regime does not outperform a buy-and-hold over the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in retail sales above a high threshold signals unsustainable consumer spending funded by credit, leading to a pullback in consumption and negative forward equity returns as mean reversion sets in.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns after RSAFS exceeds 800000 are not on average negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or the signal's information coefficient is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous three-month acceleration in industrial production and consumer price inflation indicates supply-side constraints driving up costs without corresponding demand growth, a stagflationary mix that erodes corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, the average forward 20-day return is not negative or the joint signal adds less than 100 basis points of annualized excess return over a single-series strategy.

mechanism_narrative: A high level of real GDP indicates strong aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings and supports the equity risk premium, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as the economic expansion continues.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between GDPC1 levels and forward 60-day returns is negative or below 0.03 over any ten-year period, or long positions following high GDP prints underperform a simple time-series momentum.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4%, the labor market becomes extremely tight, triggering wage inflation fears that cause the Fed to accelerate tightening, leading to a quick negative repricing in equities within the following week.
failure_condition: Forward five-day returns after UNRATE dips below 4% are not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.10), or the signal's hit rate does not exceed 55% over the testing period.
